{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment investigates conditioned place preference (CPP) in mice, focusing on the effects of cocaine administration and the role of Cav1.2 mRNA expression. The goal is to understand the acquisition, extinction, and reinstatement of CPP in C57BL/6J mice, with a particular emphasis on the impact of AAV-GFP and AAV-Cre treatments on Cav1.2 mRNA levels in the nucleus accumbens core (NAc CORE) and shell (NAc SHELL).",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "Pre-test: Initial assessment of mouse behavior in a neutral environment.",
    "Conditioning: Mice are administered 10 mg/kg cocaine intraperitoneally (i.p.) in the morning and saline in the afternoon for three days.",
    "Acquisition Test: Evaluation of CPP after conditioning.",
    "Extinction: Gradual reduction of CPP over several days.",
    "Reinstatement: Re-administration of 10 mg/kg cocaine to test for the return of CPP.",
    "Data collection and analysis: Graphical representation of cocaine-paired minus saline-paired time, and Cav1.2 mRNA expression levels."
  ],
